title: Catamaran Charter Day in Fuengirola: Costs and Tips 2026
html:
A catamaran charter day in Fuengirola gives a stable, sun-soaked cruise along the Costa del Sol, setting off from Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola with day prices typically banding from EUR 600 to EUR 2,000 depending on size and season. BoatHire24 lists local catamarans you can compare and book.

Where to set off in Fuengirola

Most catamaran days begin at Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, the town marina just below the Sohail Castle. From there the coast opens out west towards Marbella and east towards the wider Bay of Malaga, with several sheltered swim stops within easy reach.

  • Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola: the main departure marina, with berths, fuel and a long boardwalk of restaurants.
  • Castillo Sohail beach: a calm anchorage just outside the harbour mouth, popular for a first swim.
  • Carvajal and Torreblanca coves: quieter strips of sand a short cruise east along the bay.
  • Benalmadena and Puerto Marina: a striking neighbouring port roughly five nautical miles east.

Which catamaran to rent in Fuengirola?

For a stable, shaded day with room to move, a sailing or power catamaran in the 40 to 70 foot range suits most groups in Fuengirola. Catamarans sit flatter than monohulls, which keeps drinks upright and guests comfortable. The choices below run from compact day boats to large skippered platforms.

  1. Small motorboat: best for couples or a quick hour or two, seating up to 6 guests, from EUR 120.
  2. Sailing catamaran (40 to 45 ft): ideal for a relaxed family day with shade and a swim ladder, up to 10 guests, from EUR 700.
  3. Large day catamaran (50 to 70 ft): made for celebrations and roomy sun decks, around 12 guests, with the Shangani Blue Lagoon 70 ft charter page worth a look for bigger parties, from EUR 1,400.
  4. Power catamaran: faster transfers between bays without losing deck space, up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,200.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: a sleeker option for those who prefer a planing hull and a skipper, around 10 guests, from EUR 1,800.

How much does a catamaran charter day in Fuengirola cost?

Prices move with boat size, crew and the calendar, but the bands below reflect a typical full or half day on this stretch of the Costa del Sol. You can scan live day charters in Fuengirola to match these figures against real availability for your dates.

  • A shared catamaran tour seat usually costs from EUR 35 to EUR 60 per person.
  • A small motorboat half-day commonly runs from EUR 250 to EUR 450.
  • A catamaran full-day generally sits between EUR 700 and EUR 1,600.
  • A crewed yacht for the day often ranges from EUR 1,800 upwards.

When is the best season to charter in Fuengirola?

The high season for a catamaran day in Fuengirola runs from June to September, when seas are warmest and skies are reliably clear. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and tend to be cheaper. Local breezes such as the easterly Levante and the westerly Poniente shape which bay reads calmest on the day.

Book a morning start in July and August: the water is glassiest before the afternoon Levante builds, and you will share the swim coves with fewer boats than at midday.

Spain generally asks a recognised licence to skipper a powered boat yourself, though many catamarans here come with a professional skipper, so no licence is needed by guests. The operator confirms exact requirements for each listing before you pay.

How does booking a catamaran in Fuengirola work?

You pick your dates, compare verified catamarans, then reserve online with the price and inclusions shown upfront. Listings set out capacity, fuel policy and whether a skipper is included, so there are no surprises at the pontoon. Messaging the host beforehand lets you confirm the route, swim stops and any catering for the day.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 600 to EUR 2,000 for a typical catamaran day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: roughly 4 to 8 hours for a half or full day
  • Capacity: around 10 to 12 guests on a day catamaran
  • Departure point: Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Fuengirola

Do I need a licence to charter a catamaran in Fuengirola?

No licence is required when you book a skippered catamaran, as a qualified captain handles the boat. If you intend to drive a powered vessel yourself, Spain expects a recognised certificate, and the operator will confirm the precise rules.

How many people fit on a day catamaran?

Most day catamarans in Fuengirola carry around 10 to 12 guests comfortably, with the larger 70 foot platforms taking the upper end. Capacity is always stated on each listing, so you can match the boat to your group before confirming.

What is included in a catamaran charter day?

A typical day includes the skipper, fuel for the agreed route and safety equipment, with swim gear often on board. Catering, drinks and watersports may be added, and each listing makes clear exactly what the quoted price covers.

How far in advance should I book?

For July and August, booking two to four weeks ahead is sensible, as the best catamarans and weekend slots fill early. Outside high season you can often secure a boat within a few days of your chosen date.

A catamaran day from Fuengirola is one of the easiest ways to enjoy the Costa del Sol in 2026, with calm decks, warm swim stops and prices to suit most groups. Compare boats and dates on the BoatHire24 marketplace, and owners with a spare hull can list it alongside the local fleet.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fuengirola and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
